title: Tandem Contract
description: Written by the backend agent after implementation. Read by the frontend agent. Describes the actual API surface that was built — not what was planned. Every field must reflect reality.

ticket_id.description: The ticket ID this contract belongs to. Must match the ticket that triggered the backend agent run.

be_commit.description: Full or short git SHA of the backend commit that implements this feature. The frontend agent uses this to verify it is building against the right backend version.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the backend agent wrote this contract.

base_url_hint.description: Optional. The path prefix for all endpoints in this contract. Example: '/api/v1/users'. Frontend agent uses this when constructing API calls.

auth.description: Authentication scheme used by all endpoints in this contract, unless an endpoint overrides it.

type.description: The auth mechanism. 'bearer' = Authorization: Bearer <token>. 'api-key' = custom header. 'session-cookie' = HttpOnly cookie. 'none' = public endpoint.

header.description: For api-key auth: the header name. Example: 'X-API-Key'.

notes.description: Any notes the backend agent wants the frontend to know about auth. Example: 'Token expires in 1 hour. Refresh via POST /auth/refresh'.

endpoints.description: Every HTTP endpoint this feature introduces. One object per endpoint.

path.description: Path relative to base_url_hint. Path params in :colon notation. Example: '/users/:userId/profile'.

summary.description: One sentence. What this endpoint does. Example: 'Returns the authenticated user's profile including avatar and bio'.

auth_override.description: If this endpoint uses a different auth scheme from the contract-level auth, set it here.

request.description: The shape of the incoming request. All optional — only include what this endpoint actually accepts.

params.description: URL path parameters as key → TypeDef. Example: { "userId": { "type": "string", "format": "uuid" } }

query.description: Query string parameters as key → TypeDef.

body.description: Request body shape. Use $ref to a named type in the types map when the body is a known model.

headers.description: Non-auth headers the frontend must send. Key is header name.

response.description: All possible response shapes from this endpoint.

status.description: HTTP status code on success. Usually 200 or 201.

body.description: The response body shape on success.

errors.description: All error responses this endpoint can return. Include every status code the frontend must handle.

code.description: Machine-readable error code. Example: 'EMAIL_ALREADY_EXISTS', 'TOKEN_EXPIRED'. The frontend uses this to show the correct message.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of when this error fires.

types.description: Named TypeScript interfaces shared by request and response bodies. Keys are PascalCase type names. Values are TypeDef objects. The frontend agent uses these to write typed API calls and components.

frontend_guidance.description: Structured guidance the backend agent writes specifically for the frontend agent. Derived from what was actually built.

loading_states.description: Async states the frontend must handle. Example: ['Submit button loading while POST /users/profile is in-flight', 'Skeleton while initial GET /users/:id loads'].

optimistic_update.description: Whether the frontend should optimistically update UI before the server responds. True only if the backend guarantees the operation is idempotent or can be rolled back.

pagination.description: Pagination details if any endpoint returns a list.

strategy.description: 'cursor' = next_cursor token. 'offset' = page + per_page. 'none' = no pagination.

default_page_size.description: Default number of items returned per page.

polling_required.description: If true, the backend operation is async. Frontend must poll a status endpoint or connect to a WebSocket.

gotchas.description: Anything the backend agent discovered during implementation that the frontend must know. Examples: 'The profile image upload endpoint accepts multipart/form-data, not JSON', 'Rate limit is 10 requests per second on search endpoint', 'Password field is write-only — never returned in responses'.

be_notes.description: Free-form notes from the backend agent. Implementation decisions, trade-offs made, things left for follow-up tickets. Not structured — just whatever the agent wants to document.

TypeDef.description: A reusable inline type definition. Supports primitives, objects, arrays, unions, and $ref references to named types in the types map.

format.description: Optional. JSON Schema format. Examples: uuid, date-time, email, uri.

enum.description: If set, value must be one of these.

additionalProperties.description: For dynamic key maps (Record<string, T>). Use instead of properties when keys are not known at design time.

$ref.description: Reference to a named type in the contract's types map. Example: 'UserProfile'.
